content:
INT. OVERLOOK HOTEL/LOBBY - DAY - M.L.S.
 Jack walks L-R across Lobby. CAMERA TRACKS BACK & PANS with him to RECEPTIONIST behind desk.
Hi, I've got an appointment with Mr. Ullman. My name is Jack Torrance.
RECEPTIONIST His office is the first door on the left.
Thank you.
JACK moves away R-L. CAMERA PANS with him and TRACKS IN after him through Secretary's office to open door of ULLMAN's office - revealing ULLMAN seated at desk with SECRETARY standing beside him.
Mr. Ullman?
Yes?
I'm Jack Torrance.
Oh, well - come on in Jack.
ULLMAN stands up and hands book to SECRETARY, walking round side of desk. JACK moves into office. CAMERA TRACKS IN after him. He shakes hands with ULLMAN.
Very nice to meet you.
Nice to meet you, Mr. Ullman.
ULLMAN points to SECRETARY.
This is my secretary, Susie.
SECRETARY Hallo.
Susie, how do you do?
Have any trouble finding us?
Oh, no problem at all. I made the trip in three and a half hours.
Well, that's very good time, very good. Please sit down a minute.
ULLMAN points to chair cam.R. JACK sits cam.R.f.g. ULLMAN walks behind desk.
JAck, just make yourself at home. Would you like some coffee?
Well, if you are going to have some, I wouldn't mind. Thanks.
I don't want to go there, Mrs. Torrance.

CUT TO:
 M.S. WENDY
Well, how come you don't want to go?

CUT TO:
 M.S. DANNY wiggles forefinger of L.hand and speaks with different voice.
I just don't.

CUT TO:
 M.S. WENDY.
Well, let's just wait and see. We're all gonna have a real good time.

DISSOLVE TO:
 INT. OVERLOOK HOTEL/ULLMAN'S OFFICE - DAY - M.L.S.
 JACK over ULLMAN seated at desk. BILL WATSON enter office. JACK rises and shakes hands with him.
Bill, I'd like you to meet Jack Torrance.
How do you do?
Bill, how do you do?
Pleased to meet you.
Pleasure to meet you.
WATSON & JACK sit down.
Jack is going to take care of the Overlook for this winter. I would like you to take him around the place as soon as we are through.
Fine.
Jack is a schoolteacher.

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. JACK.
Eh - formerly a school-teacher.
OFF) What line of work are you in now?
I'm a writer um... Teaching has been more or less a way of making ends meet.

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. WATSON.
Well this ought to be quite a change for you.

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. JACK
Well, I'm looking for a change.
Let's see, where were we? Yes. I was about to explain that eh... our season here runs from oh May 15th to October 30th and then we close down completely until the following May.

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. JACK
Do you mind if I ask why you do that? It seems to me that the skiing up here would be fantastic.
OFF) Oh, it sure would be

CUT TO:
 M.S. ULLMAN

but the problem is the enormous cost it would be to keep the road to the Sidewinder open. It's a... It's a 25 mile stretch of road - gets an average of 20 feet of snow during the winter, and there's just no way to make it economically feasible to keep it clear. When the place was built in 1907, there was very little interest in winter sports, and this site was chosen for its seclusion and scenic beauty.

CUT TO:
 M.L.S. ULLMAN over WATSON & JACK.
Well, it's certainly got plenty of that.
Only in a very general way.
Well...

CUT TO:
 M.S. ULLMAN

...the winters can be fantastically cruel, and the basic idea is to...to cope with the very costly damage and depreciation which can occur. And this consists mainly of running the boiler, heating different parts of the hotel on a daily rotating basis, repairing damage as it occurs and doing repairs, so that the elements can't get a foothold.

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. JACK
Well, that sounds fine to me.

ULLMAN grunts off.
 CUT TO:
 M.S. ULLMAN
Physically, it's not a very demanding job. The only thing that can get a bit trying up here during the winter is eh... the tremendous sense of isolation.

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. JACK
Well, that just happens to be exactly what I'm looking for. I'm eh... I'm outlining a new writing project, and eh... (MORE)
five months of peace is just what I want.

CUT TO:
 M.S. ULLMAN
That's very good Jack, because eh... for some people eh solitude and isolation...

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. JACK.
OFF) can of itself be a problem.
Not for me.

CUT TO:
 M.S. ULLMAN.
How about your wife and son? How do you think they'll take to it?

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. JACK.
They'll love it.
been known to give a few people second thoughts about the job.

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. JACK
I'm intrigued.

CUT TO:
 M.S. ULLMAN.
I don't suppose they eh told you anything in Denver about the tragedy we had up here during the winter of 1970?

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. JACK shakes his head.
I don't believe they did.
He ran amok and eh... killed his family with an axe,

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. JACK
OFF) stacked them neatly in one of the rooms in the West Wing, and then he um... then he put eh both barrels of his shotgun in his mouth.

CUT TO:
 M.S. ULLMAN sitting behind desk.
The police eh... they thought that it was what the old-timers used to call cabin-fever, a kind of claustrophobic reaction which can occur when people are

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. JACK
OFF) shut in together over long periods of time.
Well, that is eh quite a story.
Ah, I certainly can, and eh... I also understand why your people in Denver left it for you to tell me.

JACK laughs.
 CUT TO:
 U.S. ULLMAN laughing.
Well, obviously some people can be

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. JACK.
OFF) put off by the idea of staying alone in a place where something like that actually happened.

Well, you can rest assured Mr. Ullman, that's not going to happen with me, and eg as far as my wife is concerned, I am sure she'll be absolutely fascinated when I tell her about it. She's a confirmed ghost story and horror film addict.
She picks up telephone receiver.
into phone) Hello.

CUT TO:
 INT. HOTEL - LOBBY - DAY - M.L.S.
 JACK leaning on counter at reception desk, speaking into phone.
into phone) Hi, babe.
over phone) Hi, hon. How's it going?
into phone) Great. Look, I'm at the hotel and I still have an awful lot to go through. I don't think I can get home before nine or ten.

CUT TO:
 INT. BOULDER APARTMENT/LIVING ROOM - DAY - M.S.
 WENDY sitting on back of chair phone to ear.
into phone) Sounds like you got the job?

CUT TO:
 INT. HOTEL - LOBBY - DAY - M.L.S.
 JACK leaning on reception desk with phone to ear.
into phone) Right it's a beautiful place. You and Danny are gonna love it.

EXT. COLORADO MOUNTAINS - DAY - L.S.
 High Angle Trees on side of Mountain - CAMERA TRACKS FORWARD over them to Jack's car moving away along road.
 CUT TO:
 INT. JACK'S CAR - DAY - M.S.
 WENDY sitting beside JACK as he drives along Mountain Road. DANNY, between them, is leaning on backs of their seats. WENDY yawns.
Boy, we must be really high up. The air feels so different.
Uh...huh.
Dad?
Yes?
I'm hungry.
Well you should have eaten your breakfast.
We'll get something as soon as we get to the hotel, okay?
Okay, Mom.
Hey, wasn't it around here that the Donner party got snowbound?
I think that was farther west in the Sierras.
There were a party of settlers in the covered wagon times. They got snowbound one winter in the mountains. They had to resort to cannabilism in order to stay alive.
You mean they ate each other up?
They had to, in order to survive.
Jack...
Don't worry, Mom. I know all about cannabilism, I saw it on T.V.
See, it's okay. He saw it all on the television.

Two Girls carrying luggage enter cam.R and walk away to b.g.
 GIRLS Goodbye Mr. Ullman.
Goodbye.
ULLMAN & WATSON walk L-R to JACK sitting in chair.
Good morning Jack. I hope you haven't been waiting too long.
No problem. In fact we had time to grab a bite to eat.
JACK stands up.
Good. Glad you made it before they shut down the kitchen. Is your family having a look around?
No, my son's discovered the games room.
Oh... Has your luggage been brought in?
JACK points to luggage.
Right there.
WATSON walks out cam.L.
I'd better collect my family first.
Oh...

ULLMAN laughs and they move out cam.L.
 DISSOLVE TO:
 INT. HOTEL/COLORADO LOUNGE - DAY - M.L.S.
 ULLMAN, followed by WENDY, JACK & WATSON, moves out of lift cam.R. They walk R-L across Lounge. CAMERA TRACKS with them.
This is our Colorado Lounge.
Oh, it's beautiful. (to Jack) My God, this place is fantastic, isn't it hon?
Sure is.
GIRLS Goodbye Mr. Ullman.
Goodbye girls.
ULLMAN waves to Girls and moves cam.R into JACK's apartment. WENDY & JACK follow him.
And here are your quarters.

CUT TO:
 INT. HOTEL/JACK'S APARTMENT - DAY - M.S.
 ULLMAN, followed by WENDY and JACK, move up steps. ULLMAN points to open door cam.R.
Living Room, bedroom, bathroom, and a small bedroom for your son.
JACK leans forward and looks in small bedroom cam.L. He turns and looks cam.R. at ULLMAN.
Perfect for a child.
Yeah.
Yes.
They move into apartment. JACK & WENDY move away through living room into bedroom - CAMERA TRACKS FORWARD after them.
OFF) Well the place is very nicely self- contained, easy to keep.
JACK feels bed.
Cosy.
OFF) Yes, very cosy for a family, and if you feel like spreading out you have the rest of the hotel to move around in.
Well, it's very... homely.
I'm afraid you're not going to do too well here unless you've brought your own supplies. We always remove all the booze from the premises when we shut down:
He points to shuttered bar.
that reduces the insurance that we normally have to carry.
DICK HALLORAN walks forward from b.g.
We don't drink.
ULLMAN laughs.
Well then, you're in luck.
ULLMAN waves to HALLORAN/
Oh Dick, come on over and say hello to Mr. and Mrs. Torrance.
Sure.
This is Dick Halloran, our Head Chef.
JACK shakes hands with DICK.
Mr. Halloran, I'm Jack, and this is my wife, Winifred.
The Torrances are going to take care of the Overlook for us this winter.
Oh, that's just great. How do you folks like our hotel so far?
Oh it's just wonderful.
WENDY waves out cam.L.
Hi Danny!

CUT TO:
 INT. HOTEL - BALLROOM - DAY - M.S.
 SUSIE holding DANNY's hand. They walk L-R across Ballroom. CAMERA TRACKS with them to JACK, HALLORAN, WATSON, WENDY & ULLMAN.
 SUSIE I found him outside looking for you.
OFF) Danny, did you get tired (IN SHOT) of bombing the universe?
Dick, if you're ready to do it now, I think it would be a good idea if you could show Mrs. Torrance the kitchen, while I continue on with Jack.
It will be a pleasure. Right this way Mrs. Torrance.
Great. See you later, hon.
HALLORAN walks away to b.g. WENDY & DANNY follow him.
Bye darling.
Dick, can we borrow Mrs. Torrance for a few minutes? We're on our way through to the basement - I promise we won't keep her very long.
No problem, Mr. Ullman. I was just getting to the ice cream.
HALLORAN leans down to DANNY.
Do you like ice cream, Doc?
Yeah.
HALLORAN laughs.
I thought you did.
HALLORAN straightens up and looks at JACK.
You folks don't mind if I give Danny some ice cream, while we're waiting for you?
Not at all.

CAMERA TRACKS FORWARD after him.
 CUT TO:
 INT. HOTEL - CORRIDOR TO TORRANCE'S APARTMENT - DAY - M.S.
 WENDY enters cam.L pushing trolley. She pushes it forward along corridor. CAMERA PANS L-R with her to door of their Apartment.
 CUT TO:
 INT. HOTEL - TORRANCE'S APARTMENT - DAY - M.C.S.
 JACK asleep in bed, reflected in mirror. CAMERA TRACKS BACK. WENDY enters cam.R carrying tray. She walks forward. CAMERA TRACKS BACK with her. She puts tray down on table.
Good Morning, hon. Your breakfast is ready.
What time is it?
It's about eleven thirty.
CAMERA TRACKS FORWARD to JACK & WENDY reflected in mirror.
Eleven thirty - Jesus!
I guess we've been staying up too late.
I know it.
JACK sticks his tongue out. WENDY picks up plate and glass of orange juice.
I made 'em just the way you like 'em, sunny side up.
Hmm, nice.
It's really pretty outside. How about taking me for a walk after you've finished your breakfast?
Oh I suppose I oughta try to do some writing first.
He puts empty glass down cam.L and picks up plate of eggs and bacon.
Any ideas yet?
Lots of ideas. No good ones.
WENDY sits down cam.R.
Well, something'll come. It's just a matter of settling back into the habit of writing every day.
Yeah... that's all it is.
JACK starts to eat bacon.
It's really nice up here, isn't it?
I love it. I really do. I've never been this happy, or comfortable anywhere.

I fell in love with it right away. When I came up here from my interview, it was as though I had been here before. We...we all have moments of deja vu, but this was ridiculous. It was almost as though I knew what was going to be around every corner. Ooohhhhh...

M.L.S. Corridor. DANNY in f.g. gets off tricycle, and moves R-L to door of room 237. He looks up at number - then reaches out to door handle and turns it. Door doesn't open. He looks up at number.
 CUT TO:
 M.S. Two GRADY Girls holding hands.
 CUT TO:
 M.S. DANNY looking up at number on door. He moves L-R to his tricycle. CAMERA PANS with him. He sits on tricycle and pedals fast away along corridor.
 CUT TO:
 INT. HOTEL - LOUNGE - NIGHT - M.L.S.
 Lounge. JACK sitting back to camera typing at table. CAMERA TRACKS FORWARD onto him.
 CUT TO:
 M.C.S. JACK typing.
 CUT TO:
 M.L.S. JACK back to camera typing at table in f.g. WENDY enters cam.R.b.g. and walks forward to JACK.
Hi, hon. How's it going?
WENDY stops cam.R of him. JACK pulls page from typewriter.
Fine.
WENDY kisses him.
Get a lot written today?

CUT TO:
 M.S. JACK looking up cam.L at WENDY.
Yes.
Hey, the weather forecast said it's going to snow tonight.

CUT TO:
 M.S. JACK looking up cam.L at WENDY.
What do you want me to do about it?

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. WENDY
Ah, come on hon. Don't be so grouchy.

CUT TO:
 M.S. JACK looking up cam.L at WENDY.
I'm not being grouchy. I just want to finish my work.

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. WENDY
Okay. I understand. I'll come back later on with a couple of sandwiches for you and... maybe you'll let me read something then.

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. JACK.
Wendy, (clears throat) let me explain something to you. Whenever you come in here and interrupt me, you're breaking my concentration.
JACK hits his forehead with his hand.
you're distracting me,
He picks up sheet of paper and tears it up. Then he throws the pieces down.
and it will then take me time to get back to where I was, understand?

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. WENDY.
Yes.

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. JACK
Fine. Now we're going to make a new rule. Whenever I am in here and you hear me typing,
JACK taps typewriter keys.
or whether you don't hear me typing, whatever the fuck you hear me doing in here, when I am in here that means that I am working - that means don't come in. Now do you think you can handle that?

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. WENDY
Yes.

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. JACK
Fine. Why don't you start right now and get the fuck out of here, hmm?
Well all right. But really don't make a sound.
I won't, Mom.
DANNY stands up and runs out cam.L.
Make sure you come right back, 'cause I'm going to make lunch soon. Okay?
OFF) Okay, Mom.

DISSOLVE TO:
 INT. HOTEL - JACK'S APARTMENT - DAY - M.C.S.
 Door opens and DANNY appears. He enters room - CAMERA TRACKS BACK with him as he walks forward up steps. He looks to cam.R. CAMERA PANS L-R to JACK sitting on edge of bed. JACK looks towards camera.
 CUT TO:
 M.L.S. JACK seated on bed cam.R.f.g. DANNY standing at open door in b.g.
Can I go to my room and get my fire-engine?
Come here for a minute, first.

JACK holds out his hand to DANNY, who walks forward.
 CUT TO:
 M.S. JACK sitting on bed. DANNY enters cam.L.f.g. and stops cam.R of JACK. JACK sits DANNY on his knee and puts his arms round him, kissing him.
How's it going, Doc?
M.S. JACK over DANNY seated on his knee.
Are you having a good time?
Yes, Dad.
Good. I want you to have a good time.
I am, Dad. Dad?
Yes.
Do you feel bad?
JACK shakes his head.
No. I'm just a little tired.
Then why don't you go to sleep?
I can't. I have too much to do.
Dad?
Yes?
Do you like this hotel?
JACK smiles and looks at DANNY.
Yes I do. I love it. Don't you?
I guess so.
Good. I want you to like it here. I wish we could stay here for ever, and ever... ever.
Dad?
What?
You wouldn't ever hurt Mummy and me, would you?
What do you mean?

CUT TO:
 M.S. DANNY over JACK.
Did your mother ever say that to you - that I would hurt you?
No, Dad.
Are you sure?
Yes, Dad.

CUT TO:
 M.S. JACK over DANNY.
I love you, Danny. I love you more than anything else in the whole world, and I'd never do anything to hurt you, never... You know that, don't you, huh?
Yes, Dad.
Good.
I had... I had the most terrible nightmare I ever had. It's the most horrible dream I ever had.
It's okay. It's okay, now. Really.

CUT TO:
 M.S. JACK & WENDY
I dreamed that I... that I killed you and Danny.

CUT TO:
 M.S. JACK over WENDY.
But I didn't just kill you, I cut you up into little pieces.
He puts his hand up to his eyes.
Oh...

CUT TO:
 M.S. JACK handed up to his eyes - and WENDY.
My God, I must be losing my mind!
to himself) God, I'd give anything for a drink?
JACK sits down and puts his hands up to his face.
My goddam soul, just a glass of beer.

CUT TO:
 M.S. JACK with his hands up to his face. He lowers his hands and looks - he lowers hands to bar and smiles.
Hi Lloyd.
JACK looks cam.R then back at camera.
A little slow tonight, isn't it?

JACK laughs.
 CUT TO:
 M.S. LLOYD standing behind bar.
Yes, it is, Mr. Torrance.
LLOYD moves forward - CAMERA TRACKS BACK revealing JACK seated at bar.
What'll it be?

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. JACK

Now I'm awfully glad you asked me that, Lloyd, because I just happen to have two twenties and two tens right here in my wallet. I was afraid they were going to be there until next April. So here's what: you slip me a bottle of Bourbon, a glass and some ice. You can do that, can't you, Lloyd? You're not to busy, are you?
M.S. LLOYD
No, sir. I'm not busy at all.
LLOYD turns away to bottles of shelf.
OFF) Good man.
LLOYD turns with bottle and glass to counter.
OFF) You set them up, and I'll knock them back, Lloyd, one by one.

CUT TO:
 M.S. JACK sitting at bar. LLOYD puts bottle and glass down on bar. LLOYD puts ice in glass and fills it from bottle.
White man's burden, Lloyd my man. White man's burden.
JACK looks at his wallet then at LLOYD.
Say, Lloyd, it seems I'm temporarily light.
JACK laughs.
How's my credit in this joint anyway?

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. LLOYD
Your credit's fine, Mr. Torrance.

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. JACK
That's swell. I like you, Lloyd. I always liked you. You were always the best of them. (MORE)
Best goddamned bartender from Timbuctoo to Portland Maine - Portland Oregon for that matter.

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. LLOYD
Thank you for saying so.

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. JACK looks at his glass.
Here's to five miserable months on the wagon and all the irreparable harm that it's caused me.

He drinks and lowers his glass - then looks at LLOYD.
 CUT TO:
 M.S. JACK sitting at bar. LLOYD behind bar.
How are things going, Mr. Torrance?
Things could be better, Lloyd. Things could be a whole lot better.
I hope it's nothing serious.
JACK taps on bar and LLOYD fills up his glass.
No, nothing serious.

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. JACK
Just a little problem with the... old sperm bank upstairs.
Nothing that I can't handle though, Lloyd. Thanks.

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. LLOYD
Women! Can't live with 'em. Can't live without 'em!

CUT TO:
 M.S. JACK over LLOYD. He points finger at LLOYD.
Words of wisdom, Lloyd. Words of wisdom.
JACK drinks - then swirls drink round in glass, putting glass down on counter.
I haven't laid a hand on him. Goddam it, I didn't. I wouldn't touch one hair of his goddam little head. I love the little son-of-a- bitch.
JACK laughs.
I'd do anything for him. Any fucking thing for him.

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. LLOYD
OFF) That damn bitch.

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. JACK
As long as I live she'll never let me forget what happened!
I did hurt him once, okay? It was an accident, complete unintentional. It could have happened to anybody.
He raps on counter with hand.

And it was three goddam years ago. The little fucker had thrown all my papers all over the floor. All I tried to do was to pull him up. A momentary loss of muscular coordination. I mean... A few extra foot pounds of energy, per second... per second.
Are you out of your fucking mind?

CUT TO:
 M.S. WENDY over JACK.
No. It's the truth, really. I swear it. Danny told me. He went up into one of the bedrooms, the door was open, and he saw this crazy woman in the bath-tub. She tried to strangle him.

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. JACK.
Which room was it?
OFF) Yes, it's me.
She moves R-L. CAMERA PANS with her down steps to front door. CAMERA TRACKS FORWARD after her.
Oh, thank God!
WENDY takes chain off door and opens it. JACK enters apartment.
Did you find anything?
No, nothing at all.
JACK closes front door.
I didn't see one goddam thing.
JACK & WENDY walks forward and move up steps to DANNY's bedroom. CAMERA TRACKS BACK before them.
You went into the room Danny said - to 237?
Yes, I did.
And you didn't see anything at all?
Absolutely nothing. How is he?
JACK moves R-L and looks into darkened bedroom.
He's still asleep.
Good.
JACK closes bedroom door and turns to WENDY.
I'm sure he'll be himself again in the morning.
They move L-R into Living Room. CAMERA PANS with them and TRACKS after them across room.
Well, are you sure it was the right room? I mean, maybe Danny made a mistake?
WENDY & JACK move into their bedroom.
He must have gone into that room - the door was open and the lights were on.
WENDY sits on bed.
Oh, I just don't understand.

JACK sits down on bed beside her.
 CUT TO:
 M.C.S. WENDY sobbing.
Well what about those bruises on his neck?

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. JACK over WENDY.
Somebody did that to him.
I think he did it to himself.
Wendy, once you rule out his version of what happened, there is no other explanation - is there? It wouldn't be that different from the episode that he had before we came up here, would it?

CUT TO:
 INT. OVERLOOK HOTEL - DANNY'S BEDROOM - M.S.
 DANNY lying awake on his bed. CAMERA TRACKS IN on him.
OFF) Jack, whatever the explanation is,

CUT TO:
 M.S. Low Angle Door with word "MURDER" written backwards across it.
 CUT TO:
 M.C.S. DANNY lying awake on his bed. CAMERA TRACKS IN on him.
OFF) I think we have to get Danny out of here.

CUT TO:
 INT. JACK'S APARTMENT - BEDROOM - M.C.S.
 JACK over WENDY.
Get him out of here?
Yes.
You mean just leave the hotel?

INT. DANNY'S BEDROOM - M.C.S.
 DANNY with his mouth wide open.
 CUT TO:
 INT. HOTEL - LOBBY - M.S.
 Blood gushing forward from lifts and surging up into camera lens.
 CUT TO:
 INT. HOTEL - JACK'S APARTMENT - M.C.S.
 JACK over WENDY.

It is so fucking typical of you to create a problem like this when I finally have a chance to accomplish something. When I'm really into my work. I could really write my own ticket if I went back to Boulder now, couldn't I?

CUT TO:
 M.S. WENDY & JACK sitting on bed - JACK stands up and turns to her.
Shovellings out driveways, work in a car wash - any of that appeal to you?
Jack...
Wendy, I have let you fuck up my life so far, but I'm not going to let you fuck this up!

RANGER puts phone down.
 CUT TO:
 INT. HOTEL CORRIDOR & BALLROOM - M.L.S.
 JACK walks forward along corridor to Ballroom. He turns L-R into Ballroom - CAMERA TRACKS with him revealing crowded Ballroom. MAITRE D standing at entrance.
 MAITRE D Good evening, Mr. Torrance.
Good evening.
JACK moves L-R across crowded Ballroom - CAMERA TRACKS with him to LLOYD serving behind bar. JACK sits at bar. LLOYD moves to him.
Hi Lloyd. I've been away. Now I'm back.
Good evening Mr. Torrance.

LLOYD puts dishes of olives and peanuts on bar before JACK.
 CUT TO:
 M.S. JACK over LLOYD.
It's good to see you.
It's good to be back, Lloyd.

CUT TO:
 M.C.S. LLOYD
What'll it be, sir?

CUT TO:
 M.S. JACK over LLOYD
Hair of the dog that big me.
M.C.S. LLOYD
Bourbon on the rocks.

CUT TO:
 M.S. JACK over LLOYD
That'll do her.

JACK takes handful of peanuts and empties them into his mouth. LLOYD puts ice into glass.
 CUT TO:
 M.C.S. LLOYD preparing drink.
 CUT TO:
